decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C) by ~4.26°C. Previous attempts to increase spreader conductivity hit the upper bound (500.0). The optimization history suggests shifting to geometry changes. Increasing the heat spreader height is the most effective remaining lever to improve vertical thermal conduction from the chip to the spreader without violating domain bounds or overlap constraints.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader height by 50% (within 1.5x limit) increases the cross-sectional area for heat flow from the chip into the high-conductivity spreader, reducing thermal resistance and lowering peak temperature.

expected_effects: Improved heat spreading efficiency due to larger thermal mass and contact area
risk_notes: Ensure the new y0 + height (0.32 + 0.15 = 0.47) remains within the design domain height (0.5).
Verify no geometric overlap occurs with the chip (chip top is at 0.32, spreader bottom is at 0.32; they are adjacent, so increasing height moves the top up, which is safe).
